title: Krayiss II

{{Quote|Other Jedi have come here before you. Do not think you are the first, or will be the last.|A Sith spirit, to Vara Nreem|<ref name="darkside" />}}
'''Krayiss II''', also referred to as '''Krayiss Two''', was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]], in the remote [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]] region of the [[Stygian Caldera]] [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]]. Connected to other nearby worlds by the [[Nache Bhelfia]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]], Krayiss II was considered one of the five sacred [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] planets that formed the heart of the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]]. In ancient [[Time/Legends|times]], Krayiss II was home to a [[Krayiss Two library-temple|massive library-temple]] that was a place of [[Meditation/Legends|meditation]] and study for [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith sorcerers]]. However, after [[14,000 BBY]], it was buried and largely forgotten, with its [[Krayiss Obelisk|large obelisk]] being all that remained above ground. Still, Krayiss II attracted a number of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] seeking to learn the secrets of the ancient temple. In [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]], a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] named [[Vara Nreem]] traveled to the world for this purpose, but she was deemed unworthy by the Sith [[Force ghost/Legends|spirits]] that dwelled within the ruin. As a result, Nreem was [[Death/Legends|killed]] and made to suffer in torment for eternity.

==Description==
{{Quote|And down we go! This is just like that excavation on Krayiss—without the insane Force apparitions, of course.|Talos Drellik, on the tunnels of [[Makeb]]|<ref name="ROTHC">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Rise of the Hutt Cartel]]''</ref>}}
Located in the [[Esstran sector/Legends|Esstran sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="atlas" /> Krayiss II,<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref><ref name="FaD CR">[[Star Wars: Force and Destiny Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Force and Destiny'' Core Rulebook]]</ref> also known as Krayiss Two,<ref name="darkside" /> resided in the<ref name="atlas" /> [[Krayiss Two system|Krayiss system]]<ref name="companion" /> of the [[Stygian Caldera]] [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]].<ref name="atlas" /> Krayiss II's region of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] was within the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]],<ref name="companion" /> which formed the heart of the ancient [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]]. Krayiss II was considered one of the five sacred [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] planets, along with [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]], [[Khar Delba]], [[Rhelg/Legends|Rhelg]], and [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. Those five planets were linked by the looping [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] called the [[Nache Bhelfia]]—Krayiss II fell between Khar Delba and the lesser world [[Ch'hodos]] on the route.<ref name="atlas" /> Krayiss II was [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] by two [[Moon/Legends|moons]], the lowest of which hung relatively close to the planet's surface. During Krayiss II's night cycle, the moons gave the world a source of meager illumination.<ref name="darkside" />

==History==
===Early history===
{{Quote|Our work identified the looping hyperspace lanes within the Stygian Caldera that contain the planets of the Sith Empire. The Nache Bhelfia route links Ziost, Khar Delba, Rhelg, Krayiss, and Korriban—the five worlds most sacred to the Sith.|Sorzus Syn, ''Book of Sith''|<ref name="bookofsith" />}}
In the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before [[14,000 BBY]],<ref name="darkside" /> surviving members of the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Colonist/Legends|colonized]] Krayiss II with [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Starship/Legends|vessels]], [[Sith Empire expansion|integrating]] the planet into the [[Sith Empire (Pre-Republic)|ancient Sith Empire]].<ref name="bookofsith" /> A [[Krayiss Two library-temple|Sith library-temple]] was founded on the planet, a place for [[Sith sorcery/Legends|magicians]] of pure Sith blood to gather to [[Meditation/Legends|meditate]] and set down the knowledge they had gained from their experiments. However, the temple became abandoned circa 14,000 BBY, although the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirits]] of those Sith who inhabited the place still remained.<ref name="darkside" /> In [[6900 BBY]],<ref name="JvS" /> Jedi [[Exiles]] [[Sorzus Syn]] and [[Remulus Dreypa]] mapped the looping hyperlane called the Nache Bhelfia, that connected five sacred Sith planets, which included Krayiss II, along with Ziost, Khar Delba, Rhelg, and Korriban. Sorzus Syn later wrote of the planet in her [[Sorzus Syn's chronicle|chronicle]], which was later collected by [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] into his ''[[Book of Sith]]''.<ref name="bookofsith" />

===Great Sith War===
{{Quote|And I have come to complete my learning by mastering the techniques of the Sith methods for using the Force—not the dark side of the Force—just the Force.|Vara Nreem, describing her motives for traveling to Krayiss II|<ref name="darkside" />}}
By [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]], the entire temple complex was completely buried, save for the [[Krayiss Obelisk|massive obelisk]] that extended from the top of the structure. Over those ten thousand years, various [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], including several [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]], became interested in the obelisk and the power and knowledge supposedly held on Krayiss II. However, in each case, the Sith ghosts within deemed them unworthy and trapped their spirits at the [[library-temple]] to suffer in torment for eternity.<ref name="darkside" /> In [[4008 BBY]],<ref name="jev">[[Andur Sunrider]] leaves [[H'ratth]] for [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]] in [[3999 BBY]], per [[Tales of the Jedi 3|''Tales of the Jedi'' 3]]. The ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' says that two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior ([[4001 BBY]]), Sunrider went to H'ratth, and six years prior to that ([[4007 BBY]]), he saw the [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]] of his grandfather Jev, who the ''Companion'' says disappeared on a mission a year prior to that ([[4008 BBY]]). Starting at 3999 and counting back the number of years (2, then 6, then 1), one arrives at 4008 BBY, which therefore must be the year of Jev's death, given that he appeared to Andur in ghost form a year later (4007 BBY).</ref> a [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] force gathered on Krayiss II, attracting the attention of [[Jev Sunrider]], [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|Jedi Watchman]] of the [[Darada system/Legends|Darada system]]. Along with three Jedi Masters, Sunrider journeyed to the world to combat the force, but he never returned.<ref name="TalesCompanion" />

In 4000 BBY, a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] named [[Vara Nreem]] arrived on Krayiss II, seeking the knowledge of the dark side that had lured so many other Jedi in the past. Nreem made her way to the obelisk that stood on the site of the ancient library-temple and recited an incantation that awoke the Sith spirits within from their slumber and prompted the entire temple complex to rise to the surface. Seeking to study within, Nreem beseeched the spirits to allow her access to the temple, hoping to confuse them with an unusual view of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. However, the spirits were not fooled and set upon the Jedi. Although Nreem attempted to resist, she was swiftly [[Death/Legends|killed]] and her spirit made to suffer for eternity.<ref name="darkside" /> Sometime before the [[Galactic War]] between the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|restored Sith Empire]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the Empire conducted an excavation on Krayiss II that was plagued by the planet's Sith spirits.<ref name="ROTHC" /> Knowledge of Krayiss II's Sith history survived for at least several more millennia. In [[44 ABY]], Krayiss II was on a list of Sith worlds assembled by a group of Jedi led by [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], who were trying to track down a [[Sith Meditation Sphere]] called [[Ship (Sith Meditation Sphere)|Ship]].<ref name="ascension">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Ascension]]''</ref>

==Inhabitants==
In ancient [[Time/Legends|times]], many [[Blood/Legends|pureblood]] Sith sorcerers called Krayiss II home, studying and meditating at the library-temple. However, after around 14,000 BBY, the temple was forgotten, only inhabited by the spirits of those who came before. Over time, a number of Jedi, including Vara Nreem, would join them as their search for knowledge ended in disaster.<ref name="darkside" />

==Locations==
{{Quote|Taka zeech ma toka duuwaj.|Vara Nreem, reciting an incantation to gain access to the obelisk|<ref name="darkside" />}}
The main landmark of Krayiss II was its library-temple, which dated back to the ancient Sith Empire. The temple was an enormous structure of angled walls and [[pyramid]]al towers, with a tall obelisk serving as a spire on the top of the building. The temple's massive double-door was protected by a [[Force field/Legends|force field]], which only allowed certain people to enter the premises. After the temple complex became buried, the obelisk was all that remained above ground.<ref name="darkside" />

==Behind the scenes==
The planet Krayiss Two was first mentioned in ''[[A Tale from the Dark Side]]'', a short story by [[George R. Strayton]] first printed in the [[1996]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'', and later reprinted for [[Star Wars Insider 88|''Star Wars Insider'' 88]] in an online [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] supplement. The Hyperspace edition establishes the events of the story on Krayiss Two as occurring in 4000 BBY.<ref name="darkside" /> Krayiss Two was also mentioned in several other sources, including ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'',<ref name="atlas" /> ''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]'',<ref name="bookofsith" /> and [[Christie Golden]]'s [[2011]] novel ''[[Fate of the Jedi: Ascension]]''.<ref name="ascension" /> The alternate spelling "Krayiss II" was introduced in the [[2007]] reference book [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']].<ref name="JvS" />

Although the Krayiss system, and therefore the planet Krayiss II, was placed within grid square R-4 by a map of the Sith Worlds in the [[2009]] reference book ''The Essential Atlas'', the book's Appendix section listed it as being located in square R-5,<ref name="atlas" /> which was repeated in the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the book.<ref name="companion" /> This article assumes the visual map placement is erroneous and that R-5 is the correct square.
